howItWorks.intro: This screen time mental health calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Risk Score
rules.formula: Score = Total + Social Media + Before-Bed factors
rules.explanation: Composite risk from three screen time dimensions.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much screen time is too much?
faq.answer: Research suggests >2 hours of social media daily is associated with increased anxiety and depression.

faq.question: Does screen time before bed matter?
faq.answer: Yes, blue light suppresses melatonin production, delaying sleep onset by 20-60 minutes.

faq.question: Is the Screen Time Mental Health Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard mental health and wellness formulas trusted by individuals seeking balance. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What daily screen time should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate daily screen time value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Screen Time Mental Health Calculator?
sections.body: The Screen Time Mental Health Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals seeking balance who need quick, accurate calculations in the mental health and wellness space. By entering your daily screen time, social media, screen use before bed, you get instant results including impact risk level, sleep impact, recommendation. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting impact risk level right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In mental health and wellness, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals seeking balance worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to support your mental well-being with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your daily screen time and need to find the right impact risk level. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Daily Screen Time (hours)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your daily screen time — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: social media, screen use before bed, age. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your impact risk level, sleep impact, recommendation. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your daily screen time before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both impact risk level and sleep impact — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to support your mental well-being.
If you're unsure about your age, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
